english: ma γtór-ba (ma gtor ba) Glr. (a cow) emptying its udder by discharging the milk. - 3. Sch.: 'srub γtór-ba (srub gtor ba) to rend, to tear to pieces'.

strewing-oblation, an offering brought to malignant demons, either as a kind of exorcism or as an appeasing gift, in order to prevent their evil influences upon man; mčód-pa (mchod pa) daṅ (dang) γtór-ma (gtor ma) sbyín-pa (sbyin pa) to offer such an oblation, γtór-mar (gtor mar) sṅó-ba (sngo ba) to devote something for it. The ceremonies are similar to those used in sbyín-srég (sbyin sreg) Buddh. 249; the offerings consisting of things eatable and not eatable, of blood, and even of animal and vegetable feces, scattered into the air (the benefit being shared by the drí-za (dri za) q.v.). There are various sorts of Torma-offerings, according to the nature of the substances offered (ču- (chu) or čab-γtor (chab gtor), pye-γtor (phye gtor), lhag-γtor (lhag gtor), an oblation of the fragments of a meal Mil.), or according to the time at which (dgu-γtór (dgu gtor) v. dgu (dgu)), and the purpose for which they are offered (mtsún-γtor (mtsun gtor) v. mtsún (mtsun)). Other names of Torma-offerings are: blud-rgyá (blud rgya), mar - me - rgyá (mar me rgya), tìṅ-lo (?) - rgyá (ting lo rgya), ča-γsúm (cha gsum) etc. Tormas in general belong to the ceremonies most frequently performed; γtor-čá (gtor cha) are the vessels and other implements used for that purpose; γtor-sdéb (gtor sdeb) Sch.: 'a bowl for these offerings'(?). ┴ γtor-zán (gtor zan) Lex. oblation of the remnants of the daily meal to creatures of every description.

only in γtol-méd (gtol med), = ča-méd (cha med), not known, dubious, pó-am (pho 'am) mó-am (mo 'am) γtol-méd-do (gtol med do) one does not know yet, whether it will be a boy or a girl Dzl.; ći (ci) byá-baι (bya ba'i) γtol (gtol) méd (med) not knowing what to do Dzl.; gar (gar) ťál-bai (thal ba'i) γtol (gtol) med (med) not knowing where she had gone to; bdág-la (bdag la) γtol (gtol) méd (med) I do not know any thing about it Dzl. - (Sch. has a verb γtól-ba (gtol ba) to perforate, pierce; to discover, disclose; v. rtól-ba (rtol ba)).

size, width, quantity, ri-boι (ri bo'i) γtos (gtos) tsam (tsam) as high as a mountain Lex.; rim-gro (rim gro) γtos-čé-ba (gtos che ba), like rgya-čé-ba (rgya che ba), great marks of honour, extraordinary homage.
(cf. snyoms (snyoms)) complete indifference, perfect apathy (acc. to Schr. prop. 'a liberality perfectly impartial'?).
Lex. n. of a hill where Buddha was teaching.
pf. btigs (btigs), Cs. to drop, to let fall in drops, rná-bar (rna bar) sman (sman) medicine into the ear, v. °tíg-pa ('thig pa).
Glr. in rapid or close succession, *tú-tǔ-pa-la* (*thu thu pa la*) C. id.
Lex. = ruṅ (rung), fit, convenient, practicable, becoming, btúb-bo (btub bo) it is convenient etc.; btúb-pa (btub pa) v. γtúb-pa (gtub pa).

1. to wrap round, to envelop; hence 2. in W. to shut, a book, valuable books being wrapped up in a cloth before being laid by; btum-póg (btum pog) bunch or knot, produced by money and the like being tied up in the girdle.

1. = rtód-pa (rtod pa), to fasten (grazing horses or cattle) by a rope to a stake, to tedder; Mil. declares relations to be the btod-ťág (btod thag) (the tedder) in the hands of the devil. - 2. to erect, raise up, produce, cause, occasion; srol-btód- (srol btod)
